A harbor built for the next tide.

Present · 01 Brief
Decision review · 2028 horizon01 / 04

Keep the harbor working.

A three-part retrofit that absorbs tide pressure while keeping the ferry and waterfront open.

Council work session
Prepared by North Reach Studio
Existing condition02 / 04
0.74mProjected surge
612Daily ferry riders

Evidence branch · tide02A

Water arrives first here.

Pier hingeFirst operational failure at +0.51m.
Marsh shelfCan receive 38% of peak volume.
Town edgeProtect the route, not the whole shore.
Evidence branch · access02B

One route must stay open.

4.2m clearContinuous promenade width.
1:20 riseStep-free route to the ferry deck.
24 / 7Emergency access retained.
Recommended intervention03 / 04

Absorb. Lift. Connect.

Three targeted moves replace a continuous wall and preserve the harbor's working edge.

01 · Living shelf
02 · Raised pier
03 · Dry link
Requested decision04 / 04

Release the next design phase.

Approve design development with a public access checkpoint before the 60% package.

11 moDesign + permits
